# Scenario 15: Dependency Substitution (What to Do When One Component Quietly Replaces Another)

## What This Scenario Demonstrates
This scenario demonstrates swapping dependencies.

## Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    LibA -.-> LibB[lib-b:1.0.0]
    Root -- substitute --> LibB_New[lib-b-new:1.0.0]
```

### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-15-substitution/build.gradle" snippet="s15-deps"}
```

## Expected Intuition
"Replace it."

## Actual Resolution Results
*   **Gradle**: Swaps it.

## Classpath Reality
`2.0.0`.

## Why This Happens
Substitution rule.

## How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
Exclusions.

## Safer Ways to Take Control
Substitution is cleaner.

## Signals to Watch For
N/A

## How This Scales in Real Systems
Great for local development (substitute module for project).

## Key Takeaway
Swap dependencies easily.

## Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 5: Exclusions](../scenario-5-exclusions/guide.md)
